column1: Try to hit one exercise from each category each week. I'd suggest using the Lower Leg, Core and Hip Flexor categories as run warmups. I'd also suggest doing the Isometric/Plyometric category immediately after a run workout. The rest, in your 2ish lifts per week, pick 3-4  categories, one exercise from each,  3-5 sets of 3-5 reps of each exercise as a weight that you can only accomplish < 10 reps at. Highlighted exercises are my favorite, biggest bang for buck. Check box added for convience to track if you've hit that category for the week, just unchecked everyhing at the beginning of each week.

column4: Push' through the heel, keep knee from drifting forward
Go pretty heavy on weight; 'push' through the heels; don't come all the way down between reps
With medicine ball - lower med ball to feet on the 'lower' lunge portion - bring med ball overhead + march front leg at top of lunge
Pick some of these to do as a run warmup before run

column2: Side lying glute medius raise/isometric + Copenhagen plank
Med ball squeeze + lateral band walks

column4: Never let off tension (e.g. don't let legs come all the way back together), use bands on ankles + knees
Heavy weight on these, should not be able to last longer than 20 sec
Can work up to much higher height on these

column2: Shrimp Squat + single leg broad jump
